name: Pi
description: Minimal, extensible terminal harness for AI coding agents.

developer.comment: Built by Earendil Inc. and contributors; created by Mario Zechner (badlogic, of libGDX). Repo earendil-works/pi (formerly badlogic/pi-mono).

first-release.comment: GitHub repo createdAt 2025-08-09; no separate dated public announcement found, so using repo creation as first-release proxy. Checked 2026-06-05.

status.comment: Actively developed; latest release v0.78.1 around June 2026 with frequent releases. Checked 2026-06-05.

license.comment: MIT License per repo metadata and README.

specialization.comment: Unopinionated general-purpose coding harness; no narrow niche positioning.

interface.comment: Terminal coding harness with interactive TUI, print/JSON, RPC, and SDK modes — all terminal/CLI.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal; no IDE host.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Standalone terminal CLI tool; not an editor or extension.

default-model-provider.comment: Provider-agnostic; supports 15+ providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, Bedrock, Mistral, Groq, Cerebras, xAI, OpenRouter, Ollama, etc.). No single default flagship.

model-choice.comment: Switch models mid-session via /model command or keyboard shortcuts; cycle favorites with Ctrl+P.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Authenticate via API keys or OAuth/subscription per provider.

multi-file-editing.comment: Core tools read/write/edit/bash let the agent edit any number of files across a task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Runs agentic tasks autonomously; user can steer by submitting messages while the agent works.

terminal-command-execution.comment: bash is one of the four built-in tools.

mcp-support.comment: Explicitly no built-in MCP; the docs suggest building an extension to add MCP support, so not shipped.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Sub-agents intentionally excluded from core; can be approximated via tmux or a custom extension only.

codebase-indexing.comment: No semantic index/embeddings; files are read on demand into context.

planning-mode.comment: Plan mode intentionally skipped from core; can be built as an extension.

multimodal-input.comment: Supports image input (drag image onto terminal).

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Sub-agents are intentionally excluded from Pi's core ('No sub-agents... spawn pi instances via tmux, or build your own with extensions'), so there is no model-driven delegation to subagents at all. Per guideline, false because Subagents/Parallel Agents is also false. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive TUI chat plus autonomous agentic runs with steering; no inline completion and no detached async/background runner in core.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal harness with no as-you-type tab completion.

background-async-execution.comment: Runs in the foreground terminal session; no built-in fire-and-forget background/cloud execution.

persistent-memory.comment: Sessions persist as JSONL tree files and can be resumed; AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md project instructions carry across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Session tree (/tree) navigates conversation history, but working-tree git checkpointing/auto-commit is only available via an extension, not built in. Per guideline, extension-based rollback does not count.

git-integration.comment: No native git awareness in core (staging/diff/commit); git checkpointing/auto-commit is an extension. /share exports sessions to GitHub gists but is not git-tree integration.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md) and SYSTEM.md from global and project directories.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Aggressively extensible via TypeScript extensions with access to tools, commands, keyboard shortcuts, events, and the full TUI.

permission-model.comment: No built-in permission popups; tools run without approval. Docs recommend running in a container; custom confirmation flows must be built via extensions.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Open-source CLI installed locally (curl/PowerShell/npm/pnpm/bun); runs entirely on the user's machine.

privacy-mode.comment: Local open-source harness with no vendor backend; data goes only to the model provider the user configures. Training policy is the chosen provider's, not Pi's.

enterprise-controls.comment: Single-user open-source CLI; no SSO/RBAC/audit/VPC governance features.

pricing-model.comment: The tool is free and open source; users pay only their own model-provider costs (bring your own key/subscription).

free-tier.comment: Entire tool is free (MIT); only model usage is paid by the user.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free open-source tool with no paid plan for the agent itself; model API costs are separate.

maturity.comment: Young project (repo created Aug 2025), rapid pre-1.0 releases (v0.78.1), usable but evolving and minimal by design. Checked 2026-06-05.

community-size.comment: ~60k GitHub stars on the monorepo but a young, single-vendor niche harness with a small user community relative to established agents. Star count checked 2026-06-05.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Third-party Pi packages, extensions, and skills are distributed via npm; ecosystem is emerging.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md) from global and project directories.

memory-scopes.comment: Instruction files loaded from global (user) and project directories. No enterprise or per-folder scoping documented.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Only always-on AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md instruction files; no conditional path/file-type/agent-requested rule activation documented.

auto-memory.comment: No automatic cross-session memory the agent writes itself; sessions persist and compaction summarizes, but the agent does not autonomously record durable memories. Could be added via an extension.

skill-support.comment: Supports skills (Agent Skills standard) — progressive-disclosure capability packages loaded on demand.

skill-invocation.comment: Invoked via /skill:name by the user or automatically by the agent.

always-on-surface.comment: Skills use progressive disclosure — loaded on demand, with full body pulled in only on invocation.

skill-distribution.comment: Third-party Pi packages (skills/extensions) distributed via npm registry; skills can also be placed as local files. No curated marketplace.

sandboxing-technology.comment: No built-in isolation; tools run with the user's full privileges. Docs explicitly say 'No permission popups - run in a container', i.e. containerization is left to the user, not shipped. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Built-in gating is at the tool level only via --tools/--exclude-tools/--no-builtin-tools/--no-tools allowlists. No per-command, command-prefix, per-path, or per-domain gating in core; finer control requires a custom extension. Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: No declarative permission rule engine with allow/deny precedence; --tools/--exclude-tools is a flat tool allowlist, not a precedence model. Arbitrary precedence could be built in an extension but, per guideline, programmable hooks do not inflate this value. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: No built-in declarative permission policy, but permission gates, path protection, and confirmation flows are implemented as TypeScript extensions — a code-level decision point that can inspect each action. Highest rung reached is programmable. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No default secret/.env protection out of the box; an example extension demonstrates path protection but it is opt-in, not shipped-on. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: No built-in approval prompts and thus no remembered/saved allow rules; the only allowlisting is the static --tools flag set per invocation, not interactively remembered approvals. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: Pi runs with no approval prompts by default — effectively always unsupervised/auto-run; there is no per-action gate to bypass because none ships. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: No built-in egress control; the bash tool runs with the user's full network access. Any restriction would come from running inside a user-supplied container, not from Pi. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box, Pi applies edits and runs bash commands without asking — 'No permission popups'. First-run experience is full auto-run. Checked 2026-06-12.
